title: Sago Palm Poisoning in Cats: Emergency Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: Sago palms are highly toxic to cats. If your cat chewed a frond, seek immediate emergency veterinary care to prevent life-threatening liver failure.
content_html:
What is a Sago Palm?

The Sago Palm (Cycas revoluta) is a common ornamental plant often used in landscaping and as a houseplant. Although it looks like a palm tree, it is actually a cycad. Every part of this plant—from the seeds and fronds to the roots—contains a potent toxin called cycasin. This toxin is extremely dangerous to cats and can cause rapid, irreversible liver failure and neurological damage.

Is This an Emergency?

Urgency Level: High / Critical. Yes, this is a life-threatening medical emergency. If you suspect your cat has chewed on or ingested any part of a Sago Palm, you must contact an emergency veterinarian or a pet poison control hotline immediately. Do not wait for symptoms to appear. The survival rate for Sago Palm ingestion is significantly higher if treatment begins before the toxins are absorbed into the bloodstream.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If possible, take a clear photo of the plant or bring a small piece of the frond in a sealed bag to the veterinary clinic. Many plants look similar to the Sago Palm, such as the true palm or the Dracaena. A photo allows the veterinary team to quickly and accurately identify the plant, ensuring they initiate the correct life-saving treatment protocol without delay.

faqs.question: What are the early symptoms of Sago Palm poisoning?
faqs.answer: Early signs include vomiting, drooling, diarrhea, and a loss of appetite. These symptoms usually appear within 15 minutes to several hours after ingestion.

faqs.question: Can a cat survive eating a Sago Palm frond?
faqs.answer: Survival is possible if the cat receives aggressive veterinary treatment immediately. However, the mortality rate is high (estimated at 50% or more) if treatment is delayed.

faqs.question: Is the seed more toxic than the frond?
faqs.answer: While all parts of the plant are toxic, the seeds (nuts) contain the highest concentration of cycasin. However, chewing even a small portion of a frond is enough to cause fatal liver failure.
